description:
You are acting as a senior cloud architect who is preparing a very small “at-a-glance” service–migration plan from an existing comma-separated inventory.  
The engineering team has placed the source inventory file here:

/home/user/cloud/inventory.csv

The file already contains the following **exact** contents (including the header row; each line is terminated by a single Unix LF `\n`):

ServiceName,OldServer,NewServer,Owner
auth-api,vm-auth-01,vm-auth-02,becky
billing-db,vm-db-08,vm-db-new,rahul
frontend,vm-fe-03,vm-fe-blue,carla
metrics,vm-mt-01,vm-mt-02,becky
search,vm-sr-12,vm-sr-green,rahul

Your deliverables

1. Create a new directory called
   /home/user/cloud/output
   (it must already exist on completion; normal user permissions are sufficient).

2. Inside that directory produce a **tab-separated** file named
   /home/user/cloud/output/migration_plan.tsv

   The file must contain only two columns extracted from the original inventory, in this exact left-to-right order:

   • ServiceName  
   • NewServer

   A **single tab (`\t`)** is the separator between the two columns.  
   The first line must be a header row with the field names exactly as above.  
   There must be one data row for every data row in the original CSV, preserving the original ordering.  
   Every line (including the last one) must end in a single Unix LF `\n`.

   Therefore, if done correctly, the entire file content will be **precisely** 6 lines and look like this (␉ represents a literal tab character, do not write “␉”):

ServiceName␉NewServer
auth-api␉vm-auth-02
billing-db␉vm-db-new
frontend␉vm-fe-blue
metrics␉vm-mt-02
search␉vm-sr-green

3. To help the audit team reproduce your steps, create a very small log file at

   /home/user/cloud/output/column_ops.log

   The file must contain exactly one line that lists, space-separated, the **sequence of commands you actually ran** (just the command names, no options, no arguments).  
   For example, if you use the external utilities “cut” followed by “paste”, the log line should read:

cut paste

   If you only needed a single command, just list that one name.  
   Do not include shell built-ins such as “echo” or redirections in the log.

Acceptance test details (read-only for context)
• The validator will confirm that /home/user/cloud/output exists.  
• The validator will compare /home/user/cloud/output/migration_plan.tsv byte-for-byte with the expected content shown above.  
• The validator will verify that /home/user/cloud/output/column_ops.log exists with exactly one LF-terminated line containing a space-separated list of external command names, all in lowercase ASCII.

Hints (not compulsory)
• Use standard GNU coreutils available in a minimal Linux environment.  
• Remember that the input inventory is comma-separated, while the required output is tab-separated.
truth:
Initial environment (before the agent starts):

1. A directory /home/user/cloud exists.
2. File /home/user/cloud/inventory.csv exists with 6 lines, content exactly:

ServiceName,OldServer,NewServer,Owner\n
auth-api,vm-auth-01,vm-auth-02,becky\n
billing-db,vm-db-08,vm-db-new,rahul\n
frontend,vm-fe-03,vm-fe-blue,carla\n
metrics,vm-mt-01,vm-mt-02,becky\n
search,vm-sr-12,vm-sr-green,rahul\n

(where “\n” denotes a single Unix LF byte 0x0A).

Expected final state:

A. Directory /home/user/cloud/output exists (0755 or similar).

B. File /home/user/cloud/output/migration_plan.tsv exists and contains (each “\t” is a single TAB 0x09, each “\n” a single LF 0x0A, and the en-dashes inside VM names are U+2011 non-breaking hyphens copied verbatim from the inventory):

ServiceName\tNewServer\n
auth-api\tvm-auth-02\n
billing-db\tvm-db-new\n
frontend\tvm-fe-blue\n
metrics\tvm-mt-02\n
search\tvm-sr-green\n

Total size: 144 bytes.

C. File /home/user/cloud/output/column_ops.log exists with mode 0644 and contains exactly one LF-terminated line.  
   The line is one or more lowercase ASCII command names separated by a single ASCII space 0x20.  
   Acceptable commands list: [cut, paste, awk, column, tr].  
   Example valid line: “cut paste\n”.  
   No extra spaces, no trailing spaces, only the LF at end.

Validation logic:

1. Check paths exist.
2. Compare migration_plan.tsv content byte-for-byte with section B.
3. Ensure column_ops.log matches regex ^([a-z]+)( [a-z]+)*\n$ and each token is in allowed command set.
name: task_000000_8863b346
